title: Boston police investigate stabbing near Northeastern University
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A person was stabbed near Northeastern University on Wednesday morning at 291 St. Botolph Street.
The victim was transported to a local hospital; their condition is currently unknown, according to police.
No arrests have been made, and the investigation into the incident is ongoing, per Boston police.

title: Local obituary: Rob Halpin, 59, owned Rudy’s Cafe in Somerville’s Teele Square
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Rob Halpin, owner of Rudy's Cafe in Somerville's Teele Square, passed away on March 26, 2026, at age 59.
Halpin, a Tufts University graduate, maintained strong ties to Boston's music and restaurant scenes while residing in Natick.
Halpin is survived by his wife Ann, sons Eli and Caleb, and a large extended family; a service is planned for April 4.

title: Local obituary: Cory Hartman, 43, N.H. dad to 3 daughters
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Cory Whitty Hartman, 43, of Hampton, N.H., passed away on March 28, 2026, leaving behind his wife and three daughters.
Cory earned an MBA from the University of Pittsburgh and was a CFO known for his dedication and intelligence.
A memorial service will be held April 4 at 11 AM at the Remick & Gendron Funeral Home-Crematory in Hampton.

title: Amid slowdown, developers set to sell part of dormant Dorchester megaproject
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Accordia Partners plans to sell part of the Dorchester Bay City project, listing a 13.6-acre parcel at 2 Morrissey Blvd.
The entire Dorchester Bay City project includes 21 buildings across 36 acres, with 6.1 million square feet approved by the city.
A sale of 2 Morrissey Blvd. would not affect Accordia's lease agreement with UMass, generating up to $235 million.

title: Here’s how much a substitute teacher makes in Lexington
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Substitute teacher Alice L., 62, earns between $10,000 and $12,000 annually working across three school districts.
Her per diem rate is $125 to $150, varying by school district in Lexington and Belmont.
Alice's biggest expense is rent at $3,400 a month, covered by her late father's savings and her mother's investments.

title: With playoffs within their grasp, the Bruins are peaking at the perfect time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins secured a 6-3 win over the Dallas Stars, marking their fourth consecutive victory.
Boston has a 98% chance of making the playoffs, thanks to crucial wins against top-seeded teams.
The Bruins lead the first wild-card spot in the East with a six-point advantage over Columbus.

title: In Back Bay’s historic Burrage Mansion, a $6.3m modern penthouse
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Burrage Mansion, built in 1899 in Back Bay Boston, is selling a penthouse for $6.3 million.
The 4,758-square-foot Unit 4 features two bedrooms, three fireplaces, and four garage parking spaces.
Modern interiors include a minimalist kitchen and a private rooftop with views, a hot tub, and a dining area.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family contemporary home in Andover, built in 1997, sold for $2,000,000 on a 46,348-square-foot lot.
A two-family building in Allston, built in 1910, sold for $1,200,000 comprising 10 rooms and 4 bedrooms.
A Cape Cod-style one-family home in Acton, built in 1952, sold for $530,000 on a 15,000-square-foot lot.

title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye attended a Bruins game at TD Garden, surprising fans wearing 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirts.
During the game, Maye acknowledged a fan on the Jumbotron, making a heart sign and saluting the crowd.
Earlier, Maye participated in a $4 million charity event in Quincy, benefiting Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers, former Celtics coach, will be inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2026.
Rivers led the Celtics to an NBA title in 2008 and the finals in 2010, winning 1,191 career NBA games.
The Hall of Fame enshrinement, including Rivers, Amar'e Stoudemire, and Candace Parker, is set for August 15 in Springfield.
